mechanism_narrative: A high and persistent unemployment rate signals significant slack in the economy, depressing aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations. This leads to negative forward returns as investors price in a prolonged period of weak growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE value is 9.5%, well above historical averages, indicating a weak labor market.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) is positive over any 3-year rolling window when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is near the zero lower bound, it signals maximum monetary accommodation. This policy stance lowers the discount rate for future earnings and encourages risk-taking, driving equity prices higher.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest FEDFUNDS value is 0.18%, indicating the policy rate is at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in both industrial production and retail sales indicates a broad-based contraction in both the supply and demand sides of the economy. This joint signal of weakness is a strong predictor of an impending recession and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized change in INDPRO < 0 AND 3-month annualized change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: In instances where both series show negative 3-month changes, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in industrial production serves as a direct proxy for the health of the manufacturing and production sectors. Positive momentum in this series signals improving corporate operational health and earnings power, leading to positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending. This positive feedback loop into aggregate demand drives higher corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The beta of 5-day S&P 500 returns to the monthly PAYEMS surprise (actual minus consensus) is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An environment of accelerating inflation combined with a restrictive monetary policy creates a headwind for equities. Rising consumer prices (CPI) alongside a high Fed Funds rate compresses corporate margins and increases the discount rate, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive during periods when both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts is a leading indicator of a slowdown in the broader economy. This weakness in a key cyclical sector reduces demand for materials and labor, signaling lower future GDP growth and pressuring equity valuations.
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct directional predictions) of the signal for 60-day forward returns is less than 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP is the most comprehensive measure of economic activity. A sustained trend of positive real GDP growth directly translates into a favorable environment for corporate earnings growth, supporting higher equity prices over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank IC between the year-over-year percentage change in GDPC1 and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 for more than 4 consecutive quarters.
